title: Command-generation custom primitive registry and target support

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Add a schema-backed custom primitive registry and target support contract so host/domain primitives are declared, validated, implemented, or explicitly unsupported per target.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep command-generation as a maintainer dependency only; AW remains the owner of AW command meaning, generated outputs, and runtime-boundary policy.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Implementation order, helper names, and validation tightening inside this lane.
canonical_core.escalate_when: The lane needs command semantic drift, runtime dependency drift, or files outside the declared owner surfaces.
canonical_core.next_action: Model primitive definitions and target support in command-generation, then register AW domain primitives outside generic renderer branches.
completion_criteria: A custom primitive can be declared and validated.
Supported target execution uses registered implementation metadata.
Unsupported targets fail clearly; unregistered primitive ids are rejected.
AW domain primitives are modeled outside command-generation core renderer logic.

goal: Add a schema-backed custom primitive registry and target support contract so host/domain primitives are declared, validated, implemented, or explicitly unsupported per target.
non_goals: Do not implement every AW primitive in every target.
Do not allow arbitrary code references without registry validation.

intent_continuity.this slice completes the larger intended: no, it completes one child lane

execution_bounds.required validation commands: uv run python scripts/generate/generate_command_packages.py --check; uv run python scripts/check/check_generated_command_packages.py --python-conformance; uv run pytest tests/test_command_generation_artifacts.py tests/test_command_generation_primitive_executor.py -q; uv --directory ../command-generation run pytest -q
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before changing command semantics, generated package layout, runtime dependency policy, or unrelated package internals.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated AW runtime behavior, unrelated package internals, or generated outputs not covered by freshness proof.
execution_bounds.max changed files: closed slice; no further writes expected without reopening a fresh plan.

post_decomposition_delegation.reason: The current branch spans two repos and active PR context; hand off only after a lane becomes isolated and mechanical.

adaptive_assurance.reason: The epic changes ownership and dependency boundaries across AW and command-generation. Closeout gate normalized after PR #1210 merge; literal issue refs are carried in references/closure evidence, not adaptive required_refs.

immediate_next_action: Model primitive definitions and target support in command-generation, then register AW domain primitives outside generic renderer branches.
blockers: None currently known; dependency order is recorded in the decomposition.
invariants: Do not add command-generation to AW runtime dependencies.
Do not hide AW-specific behavior inside generic command_generation renderers.
Generated package proof remains the freshness and target-proof authority.
completion_criteria: A custom primitive can be declared and validated.
Supported target execution uses registered implementation metadata.
Unsupported targets fail clearly; unregistered primitive ids are rejected.
AW domain primitives are modeled outside command-generation core renderer logic.
proof_expectations: Primitive conformance covers implemented, host/domain, unsupported, and unregistered cases.
Generated package proof rejects unsupported or unregistered native primitives.
